business_ideas.idea: Brad Jacobs' platform strategy: buy fragmented small businesses, add capital and sales team.
Amorex: oil brokerage firm connecting oil producers to large buyers.
Hamilton Resource: physical oil cargo trading between countries with bank financing.
United Waste Management: consolidate fragmented landfill and trucking operators.
United Rentals: become largest equipment rental via acquisition and organic growth.
XPO Logistics: apply consolidation playbook to transportation and logistics industry.
Hire hungry sales reps with psychometric testing and incentive-heavy comp.
Quicken Loans: digitize mortgage lending, then dominate with scale and culture.
Dan Gilbert: use mortgage cash flow to fund real estate and venture bets in Detroit.
Dictionary.com: acquire and expand with games, licensing, and new verticals.

predictions.text: Brad Jacobs has founded five billion-dollar scale companies via consolidation playbook.
United Rentals market cap is now $25B, 5 years after Jacobs stepped down.
Dan Gilbert's net worth surged from $8B to $57B in two years via equity appreciation.
